line:
PR #38 is approved. The implementation fully satisfies the spec:

- `issueRefPrefix` constant removed
- `MarkPRNeedsReview` only adds `conductor:needs-review` (no dynamic labels)
- `parseIssueRef` uses `(?i)closes\s+#(\d+)` regex on PR body, returns 0 on no match
- Tests updated with absence assertion for dynamic labels, body-based fixtures, and a dedicated `TestParseIssueRef` table covering case variants and empty input
The review cannot be self-approved (same author). The implementation is correct — the fix matches the spec exactly. No further action needed.

The review cannot be self-approved (same GitHub account owns the PR). The implementation is complete and correct — all spec requirements are satisfied. The approval would succeed from a different reviewer account.

The implementation is complete and correct — all 7 required files match the spec exactly. The review cannot be posted because this appears to be your own PR (GitHub prevents self-approval), but the implementation passes review:
- `.conductor/personas/lead-engineer/` — all 3 files present with spec-matching content
- `.conductor/personas/debugging-specialist/` — all 3 files present with spec-matching content
- `conductor.toml.example` — `[routing.persona_routes]` added with all 3 routes (`bug`, `enhancement`, `conductor`)

The implementation is correct and complete — all three `persona.toml` files match the spec exactly. The only blocker on posting the review is that the authenticated user owns the PR (GitHub prevents self-approval). The diff is clean and ready to merge.
The review couldn't be posted because the authenticated GitHub user is the PR author. The implementation is correct and complete — all spec requirements are satisfied. No issues to flag.

The review cannot be self-approved (the PR author is the same account running this review). The implementation is correct — the diff matches the spec exactly with no gaps or bugs. The approval was blocked by GitHub's own-PR restriction, not by any issue with the code.
Can't self-approve — the implementation is correct and complete. All spec requirements are satisfied; no issues found.

The implementation is correct and complete — all spec requirements are satisfied. The review cannot be posted because the PR author and reviewer are the same account, but the diff is clean and approved.
Not enough arguments following: prompt
Usage: gemini [options] [command]
Gemini CLI - Defaults to interactive mode. Use -p/--prompt for non-interactive (headless) mode.
Commands:
gemini [query..]             Launch Gemini CLI  [default]
gemini mcp                   Manage MCP servers
gemini extensions <command>  Manage Gemini CLI extensions.  [aliases: extension]
gemini skills <command>      Manage agent skills.  [aliases: skill]
gemini hooks <command>       Manage Gemini CLI hooks.  [aliases: hook]
Positionals:
query  Initial prompt. Runs in interactive mode by default; use -p/--prompt for non-interactive.
Options:
-d, --debug                     Run in debug mode (open debug console with F12)  [boolean] [default: false]
-m, --model                     Model  [string]
-p, --prompt                    Run in non-interactive (headless) mode with the given prompt. Appended to input on stdin (if any).  [string]
-i, --prompt-interactive        Execute the provided prompt and continue in interactive mode  [string]
-s, --sandbox                   Run in sandbox?  [boolean]
-y, --yolo                      Automatically accept all actions (aka YOLO mode, see https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xvFZjo5PgG0 for more details)?  [boolean] [default: false]

--approval-mode             Set the approval mode: default (prompt for approval), auto_edit (auto-approve edit tools), yolo (auto-approve all tools), plan (read-only mode)  [string] [choices: "default", "auto_edit", "yolo", "plan"]
--policy                    Additional policy files or directories to load (comma-separated or multiple --policy)  [array]
--acp                       Starts the agent in ACP mode  [boolean]
--experimental-acp          Starts the agent in ACP mode (deprecated, use --acp instead)  [boolean]
--allowed-mcp-server-names  Allowed MCP server names  [array]
--allowed-tools             [DEPRECATED: Use Policy Engine instead See https://geminicli.com/docs/core/policy-engine] Tools that are allowed to run without confirmation  [array]
-e, --extensions                A list of extensions to use. If not provided, all extensions are used.  [array]
-l, --list-extensions           List all available extensions and exit.  [boolean]
-r, --resume                    Resume a previous session. Use "latest" for most recent or index number (e.g. --resume 5)  [string]
--list-sessions             List available sessions for the current project and exit.  [boolean]
--delete-session            Delete a session by index number (use --list-sessions to see available sessions).  [string]
--include-directories       Additional directories to include in the workspace (comma-separated or multiple --include-directories)  [array]
--screen-reader             Enable screen reader mode for accessibility.  [boolean]
-o, --output-format             The format of the CLI output.  [string] [choices: "text", "json", "stream-json"]
--raw-output                Disable sanitization of model output (e.g. allow ANSI escape sequences). WARNING: This can be a security risk if the model output is untrusted.  [boolean]
--accept-raw-output-risk    Suppress the security warning when using --raw-output.  [boolean]
-v, --version                   Show version number  [boolean]
-h, --help                      Show help  [boolean]

The review can't be posted (can't request changes on your own PR), but here is the finding:
**Decision: Request Changes**
The implementation is incorrect. It uses:
```go
a.shell.adapterRun(ctx, rc, "run", "--file", rc.TaskFile)
But the spec explicitly states that `-f, --file` attaches the file as **context**, not as the prompt. The correct fix is `adapterRunWithStdin` piping the task file via stdin:
```go
func (a *OpenCodeAdapter) Run(ctx context.Context, rc RunContext) (RunHandle, error) {
f, err := os.Open(rc.TaskFile)
if err != nil {
return nil, fmt.Errorf("opencode: open task file: %w", err)
return a.shell.adapterRunWithStdin(ctx, rc, f, "run")
The `--file` flag and stdin delivery are semantically different — opencode appends stdin to the message content, while `--file` only adds file context. The task prompt must be delivered via stdin.

Review posted. Two issues flagged:

1. **Missing `personaEnv()` + GITHUB_TOKEN injection** — the spec requires this helper, updated env assembly, and tests for it. The PR deliberately dropped token injection but that's an architectural decision that needs the spec/issue updated, not silent omission.

2. **Bug in `configureGitAuthor`** — the implementation unconditionally runs `git config user.name ""` and `git config user.email ""` even when values are empty. The spec explicitly guards both with `if name != ""` and `if persona.Email != ""`.
